title: Inline validation vs error summary
summary: Use inline validation to explain a specific field problem near the field. Use an error summary to help users recover from one or more form errors from the top of a page or step.
decisionRules: Use inline validation when the user can fix one field locally before page-level recovery is needed.
Use an error summary when submit-time validation leaves errors across a page or step and users need a linked overview.
Combine both after failed submit so the summary helps users find errors and inline messages explain each local correction.
failureModes: Showing only color or icons without text.
Putting all errors at the top without linking to the affected fields.
Interrupting typing with premature validation messages.

sources.note: GOV.UK error message guidance documents local field messages, preserved answers, and clear correction wording.
GOV.UK error summary guidance documents top-of-page summaries, focus behavior, and links to invalid fields.
